authors: Valley, J.W.; Ushikubo, T.; Spicuzza, M.J.
notes: LEAP; laser; zircon mineral; geoscience application; interdisciplinary
abstract: Atom probe tomography was applied to the analysis of mineral phases in geoscience, specifically to characterize the elemental distribution in zircon crystals used for geochronology. The technique enables the mapping of rare earth elements and radiogenic lead at the nanometer scale, providing information about elemental diffusion and resetting of isotopic systems in minerals. The results demonstrate the applicability of APT to geoscience problems beyond its traditional semiconductor focus.

title: Atom Probe Tomography of Semiconductor Quantum Dot Structures
authors: Molina, S.I.; Varela, M.; Pennycook, S.J.
notes: LEAP; laser; InAs/GaAs QDs; interface chemistry; composition fluctuations; 40 K
abstract: The composition and morphology of semiconductor quantum dot structures were characterized using atom probe tomography. InAs quantum dots embedded in a GaAs matrix were analyzed, revealing the three-dimensional indium distribution and the compositional transition at the QD/matrix interface. The APT results provide quantitative data on In segregation and alloying at the quantum dot surface that influence the emission wavelength and optical properties of InAs/GaAs quantum dot lasers.

title: Atom Probe Tomography of Semiconductor Nanowires: Growth Mechanism Insights
authors: Perea, D.E.; Hemesath, E.R.; Schwalbach, E.J.; Lensch-Falk, J.L.; Voorhees, P.W.; Lauhon, L.J.
notes: LEAP; laser; Si and Ge nanowires; VLS growth; catalyst incorporation; 40 K
abstract: Atom probe tomography was used to provide insight into the growth mechanism of semiconductor nanowires by the vapor-liquid-solid process. Silicon and germanium nanowires were analyzed, revealing the three-dimensional distribution of Au catalyst atoms incorporated into the nanowire body during growth. The spatial distribution of Au and other impurities provides direct evidence for the atomic incorporation pathway during VLS growth and its dependence on growth temperature and precursor pressure.

title: Atom Probe Tomography of Nitride Semiconductor Interfaces
authors: Rigutti, L.; Vella, A.; Vurpillot, F.; Gaillard, A.; Blavette, D.; Deconihout, B.
notes: LEAP; UV laser; InGaN/GaN; AlGaN/GaN; interface composition; photonic atom probe
abstract: The composition of interfaces in nitride semiconductor heterostructures was characterized using atom probe tomography. InGaN/GaN and AlGaN/GaN interfaces in LED and HEMT structures were analyzed, revealing compositional grading and alloy clustering at the atomic scale. The photonic atom probe, which combines optical spectroscopy with APT, was used to correlate local composition with optical emission properties of InGaN quantum well structures.

title: Photonic Atom Probe: Combining Optical Spectroscopy with Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Rigutti, L.; Blum, I.; Houard, J.; Vurpillot, F.; Vella, A.; Gaillard, A.; Blavette, D.; Deconihout, B.
notes: Photonic atom probe (PAP); GaN/InGaN QWs; in-situ PL during APT; UV 343 nm laser
abstract: The photonic atom probe, a novel instrument combining optical photoluminescence spectroscopy with atom probe tomography, is presented. The instrument enables simultaneous collection of the optical emission from a nanoscale specimen and the field-evaporated ion stream, allowing direct correlation between local chemical composition at the atomic scale and optical emission properties. The technique is demonstrated on GaN/InGaN quantum well structures, revealing the correlation between indium composition and emission wavelength at the nanometer scale.

title: Atom Probe Tomography of Mg-Doped GaN: Clustering and Activation
authors: Inoue, K.; Shimizu, Y.; Toyama, T.; Nagai, Y.
notes: LEAP; laser; Mg in GaN; p-type doping; 40 K; clustering and activation correlation
abstract: The distribution of magnesium dopant atoms in p-type GaN was characterized using atom probe tomography as a function of Mg concentration and annealing conditions. At high Mg concentrations, clustering of Mg atoms was observed, which is correlated with the saturation and eventual decrease in hole concentration at high Mg doping levels. The results provide atomic-scale insight into the compensation mechanism that limits the achievable hole concentration in GaN, relevant to the optimization of p-type contact layers in III-nitride devices.

title: Atom Probe Tomography of Metal/Semiconductor Interfaces in Electronic Devices
authors: Cojocaru-Mirédin, O.; Choi, P.P.; Wuerz, R.; Raabe, D.
notes: LEAP 4000; laser; metal/Si interface; contact resistance study; TiN, W contacts; 50 K
abstract: The composition and chemistry of metal/semiconductor interfaces in electronic devices were characterized using atom probe tomography. Titanium nitride and tungsten contact structures on silicon were analyzed, revealing the distribution of oxygen, nitrogen, and metal species at the contact interface. The results identify compositional features at the interface that contribute to contact resistance variability in advanced CMOS devices.

title: Atom Probe Tomography of Silicon with Laser Wavelengths from UV to Green
authors: Vella, A.; Mazumder, B.; Da Costa, G.; Deconihout, B.
notes: 257 nm, 343 nm, 515 nm comparison; Si; LaWaTAP; mass resolution and background
abstract: The effect of laser wavelength on laser-assisted atom probe tomography of silicon was investigated using three wavelengths: 257 nm (deep UV), 343 nm (UV), and 515 nm (green). The mass resolution and spectral background were measured under each condition, revealing a strong improvement in mass resolution with decreasing wavelength due to the shorter thermal transient. The optimal wavelength for silicon analysis is identified as deep UV, balancing photon absorption efficiency against laser-induced surface heating effects.

title: Atom Probe Tomography of Oxygen in Silicon: Thermal Donor Formation
authors: Inoue, K.; Shimizu, Y.; Nagai, Y.; Hasegawa, M.
notes: LEAP; laser; O in Si; thermal donors; 40 K; O clustering study
abstract: The three-dimensional distribution of oxygen in Czochralski silicon and its evolution during thermal annealing relevant to thermal donor formation were characterized by atom probe tomography. The APT analysis reveals the formation of oxygen clusters at temperatures consistent with thermal donor generation, providing direct atomic-scale evidence for the oxygen aggregation mechanism. The cluster sizes and compositions at different annealing stages are quantified and compared against predictions from diffusion-limited aggregation models.

title: Quantitative Atom Probe Tomography of Indium in Silicon: Segregation at Extended Defects
authors: Kambham, A.K.; Bogdanowicz, J.; Mody, J.; Gilbert, M.; Vandervorst, W.
notes: LEAP; laser; In in Si; dopant segregation; extended defects; 40 K
abstract: The three-dimensional distribution of indium in ion-implanted silicon was characterized by atom probe tomography, with particular attention to indium segregation at extended defects. Indium, a large misfit impurity in silicon, shows pronounced segregation to dislocation loops formed during annealing. Quantitative analysis of the In distribution at defects provides data on the trapping cross-section and segregation energy of In at silicon dislocations, relevant to understanding the activation behavior of In-doped silicon junctions.

title: Concentration Measurement of Boron in Silicon by Laser Atom Probe
authors: Kellogg, G.L.; Tsong, T.T.
notes: Early laser atom probe; Si; B concentration; field evaporation mechanism; foundational study
abstract: The concentration of boron in silicon was measured using a laser-pulsed atom probe. The technique demonstrates the detection of boron at concentrations relevant to semiconductor doping, establishing the feasibility of atom probe analysis for silicon dopant metrology. The field evaporation characteristics of Si under laser pulse conditions and the detection sensitivity for boron are quantified, providing a foundation for subsequent development of laser atom probe methods for semiconductor analysis.

title: Atom Probe Tomography of Wide-Bandgap Semiconductors Using the CAMECA EIKOS-UV
authors: Prosa, T.J.; Reinhard, D.A.; Larson, D.J.
notes: EIKOS-UV; UV laser; GaN and SiC; wide-bandgap; 30 K; extended flight path instrument
abstract: The CAMECA EIKOS-UV atom probe was applied to the characterization of wide-bandgap semiconductor materials including gallium nitride and silicon carbide. The instrument's ultraviolet laser source enables efficient photon absorption in these high-bandgap materials, while the reflectron flight path provides high mass resolution for distinguishing molecular ion species. The capabilities of the EIKOS-UV for wide-bandgap semiconductor analysis are demonstrated and the analytical performance compared against conventional LEAP instruments.

title: Atom Probe Tomography of Sub-2 nm Gate Dielectrics in Advanced Logic Devices
authors: Reinhard, D.A.; Prosa, T.J.; Larson, D.J.; Strennen, S.
notes: LEAP 5000 XR; sub-2nm gate dielectric; Si; logic node; laser-pulsed; 30 K; EUV patterned
abstract: Atom probe tomography was applied to characterize sub-2 nm gate dielectric layers in advanced logic devices patterned by extreme ultraviolet lithography. The technique resolves the distribution of nitrogen, hafnium, and oxygen across the gate dielectric stack with sub-monolayer sensitivity. The results provide critical metrology data for gate dielectric engineering at technology nodes below 2 nm, where the atomic-scale composition of the dielectric determines transistor performance and reliability.

title: Probing Grain-Boundary Chemistry and Electronic Structure in Proton-Conducting Oxides by Atom Probe Tomography
authors: Diercks, D.R.; Tong, J.; Zhu, H.; Kee, R.; Mullen, G.; Sullivan, N.P.; O'Hayre, R.; Gorman, B.P.
notes: LEAP; laser-pulsed; BaZr0.9Y0.1O3 (BZY10); grain boundary space-charge layer; 30 K
abstract: A laser-assisted atom-probe-tomographic method has been developed and applied to measure and characterize the three-dimensional atomic and electronic nanostructure at an yttrium-doped barium zirconate (BaZr0.9Y0.1O3−δ, BZY10) grain boundary. Proton-conducting perovskites are attracting interest for energy conversion applications but are hindered by high grain-boundary resistance attributed to a positive grain-boundary space-charge layer. LAAPT is used to analyze BZY10 grain-boundary chemistry in three dimensions with subnanometer resolution, and maps of the charge density and electrostatic potential arising at the grain boundaries are derived, revealing direct chemical evidence that a positive space-charge layer exists at these grain boundaries.

title: Atom Probe Tomography Specimen Preparation Using Focused Ion Beam: Methodological Advances
authors: Larson, D.J.; Prosa, T.J.; Ulfig, R.M.; Geiser, B.P.; Kelly, T.F.
notes: FIB lift-out; Si device specimens; LEAP; site-specific prep; capping layer comparison
abstract: Methodological advances in atom probe tomography specimen preparation using focused-ion beam methods are presented. Improvements to the annular milling protocol, protective capping layer deposition, and final tip sharpening procedures are described and applied to silicon device specimens. The new protocols reduce gallium implantation damage and improve the yield of successful atom probe analyses from site-specific locations in complex semiconductor device structures.
